cap-prove-224.title: Prove 2+2=4
cap-prove-224.taskPrompt: Complete the derivation and justify the rule used at each step.
cap-prove-224.openEndedPrompt: Explain which recursive addition equations justify each rewrite, and why this is a genuine PA derivation rather than 'obvious'.

criteria.description: Conceptually correct and applied to the concrete example, not memorized vocabulary.
Precise about the relevant distinction; no fatal misconception.

fatalMisconceptions.description: Treats 2+2=4 as obvious without using the recursive addition rules.

cap-proof-graph.title: Read a proof graph
cap-proof-graph.taskPrompt: Given axioms $A$, $A\to B$, $B\to C$ and two modus-ponens steps deriving $C$:
cap-proof-graph.openEndedPrompt: Identify the premises and conclusions, and explain why $C$ is reachable — and why reachability is about derivation, not truth.

criteria.description: Conceptually correct and applied to the concrete example, not memorized vocabulary.
Precise about the relevant distinction; no fatal misconception.

fatalMisconceptions.description: Treats 'true in ℕ' as 'provable in PA'.

cap-evaluate-N.title: Evaluate truth in ℕ
cap-evaluate-N.openEndedPrompt: Your answer must use: interpretation, the standard model ℕ, evaluation of +, and the equality check. Do not mention proof rules.

criteria.description: Uses interpretation of + and = in ℕ, not proof rules.
Evaluates both terms and compares, for 2+2=4 and 2+2=5.

fatalMisconceptions.description: Treats 'true in ℕ' as 'provable in PA'.

cap-distinguish.title: Distinguish syntax, proof & truth
cap-distinguish.taskPrompt: For each item say whether it is well-formed, provable in PA (assuming PA consistent), and true in ℕ — then explain the difference between $T\vdash P$ and $\mathbb{N}\models P$.
cap-distinguish.openEndedPrompt: Explain why 'well-formed', 'provable in PA', and 'true in ℕ' are three different predicates, using the four canonical examples.

criteria.description: Correctly identifies well-formedness as grammatical, independent of truth.
Explains ⊢ (derivability) and ⊨ (truth in ℕ) as different relations.
Applies the distinction correctly to all four canonical examples.
States hedges (e.g. 'assuming PA consistent/sound') where needed.

fatalMisconceptions.description: Treats 'true in ℕ' as 'provable in PA'.
Treats a malformed string as false (it has no truth value).

cap-sound-complete.title: Soundness vs completeness
cap-sound-complete.taskPrompt: Explain the difference between a theory being sound and being complete, and why a theory can be sound yet incomplete.
cap-sound-complete.openEndedPrompt: Define each precisely ($T\vdash P\Rightarrow\mathbb{N}\models P$ vs 'for every $P$, $T\vdash P$ or $T\vdash\neg P$') and give the PA example.

criteria.description: Conceptually correct and applied to the concrete example, not memorized vocabulary.
Precise about the relevant distinction; no fatal misconception.

cap-object-meta.title: Object vs metatheory
cap-object-meta.taskPrompt: Classify each claim by level, then explain why the metatheory can talk about PA's proofs.
cap-object-meta.openEndedPrompt: Explain why 'PA is consistent' is a claim about PA, and why adding Con(T) yields a stronger theory.

criteria.description: Conceptually correct and applied to the concrete example, not memorized vocabulary.
Precise about the relevant distinction; no fatal misconception.

fatalMisconceptions.description: Treats metatheoretic claims as statements inside T.

cap-computability.title: Why computable systems
cap-computability.taskPrompt: Explain why all PA theorems can be listed (r.e.) but PA theoremhood is not decidable.
cap-computability.openEndedPrompt: Distinguish proof-checking (decidable) from theoremhood (r.e., undecidable), and note why 'undecidable' is not about being slow.

criteria.description: Conceptually correct and applied to the concrete example, not memorized vocabulary.
Precise about the relevant distinction; no fatal misconception.

cap-encode.title: Encode syntax as arithmetic
cap-encode.taskPrompt: Encode the sequence $[4,7,9]$ with prime powers and explain how it is recovered.
cap-encode.openEndedPrompt: Explain how a proof becomes a number, and why the number itself does not intrinsically 'know' what it encodes.

criteria.description: Conceptually correct and applied to the concrete example, not memorized vocabulary.
Precise about the relevant distinction; no fatal misconception.

fatalMisconceptions.description: Says the code number intrinsically 'understands' the proof.

cap-prov-predicate.title: Proof-checking as arithmetic
cap-prov-predicate.taskPrompt: Explain what $\mathrm{Proof}_T(p,q)$ checks and why it is an arithmetic formula, not an English concept.
cap-prov-predicate.openEndedPrompt: Cover: the four checks $\mathrm{Proof}_T$ performs, why proof-checking is primitive recursive hence representable, and $\mathrm{Prov}_T(q):=\exists p\,\mathrm{Proof}_T(p,q)$.

criteria.description: Conceptually correct and applied to the concrete example, not memorized vocabulary.
Precise about the relevant distinction; no fatal misconception.

fatalMisconceptions.description: Says the code number intrinsically 'understands' the proof.

cap-godel-sentence.title: Construct the Gödel sentence
cap-godel-sentence.openEndedPrompt: Define $G_T$, $\mathrm{Prov}_T(q)$, and $\ulcorner G_T\urcorner$; explain why $G_T$ is still arithmetic and why this is self-reference through coding (Fixed-Point Lemma), not the liar paradox.

criteria.description: Defines G_T, Prov_T, and ⌜G_T⌝ correctly.
Explains why G_T is an arithmetic sentence about numbers.
Attributes the self-reference to the Fixed-Point Lemma / coding.
Explains why it is not the liar paradox (provability is representable; truth is not).

fatalMisconceptions.description: Says the Gödel sentence is merely the English liar paradox.

cap-first.title: Explain First Incompleteness
cap-first.openEndedPrompt: Cover: (1) the conditions on T; (2) what $G_T$ says; (3) why $T\nvdash G_T$ if T is consistent; (4) why $G_T$ is true in ℕ (soundness); (5) why this is not absolute unprovability.

criteria.description: States the three conditions on T.
Explains T⊬G_T from consistency.
Explains why G_T is true in ℕ (soundness/standard model).
Notes unprovable-in-T is not absolutely unprovable.

fatalMisconceptions.description: Says G_T is absolutely unprovable in every system.
Treats 'true in ℕ' as 'provable in PA'.

cap-second.title: Explain Second Incompleteness
cap-second.taskPrompt: Explain why a suitable consistent T cannot prove $\mathrm{Con}(T)$, and why adding it never ends the hierarchy (for a sound base, where each extension stays consistent).
cap-second.openEndedPrompt: Cover: $\mathrm{Con}(T):=\neg\mathrm{Prov}_T(\ulcorner 0=1\urcorner)$; the formalized first theorem; that a stronger theory can prove it; and why $T+\mathrm{Con}(T)$ has its own Gödel sentence.

criteria.description: States the three conditions on T.
Explains T⊬G_T from consistency.
Explains why G_T is true in ℕ (soundness/standard model).
Notes unprovable-in-T is not absolutely unprovable.

fatalMisconceptions.description: Says T can't prove Con(T) because Con(T) is false.
